what_it_does: This file is a ledger metadata record for an image asset in the design-source channel; it declares the asset id, address, path, blob reference, MIME type, dimensions, source, author session, creation time, and links, plus a one-line ground-truth caption.
observations: YAML frontmatter wraps a structured asset card for an image named p-13
The image is referenced by an image://design-source URI and a blob://b2 content-addressed blob
MIME type is image/jpeg but width and height are both 0
Source is design_source_material and author_session is ch-3mpkjg3r
The markdown body contains only the line 'SOURCE design ground-truth — presentation-15p/p-13.jpg'
standouts: Zero width and height values for an image asset
A blob://b2 content-addressed reference paired with a channel URI
Explicit 'SOURCE design ground-truth' label in the body
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter followed by a short markdown body
Follows an asset ledger schema with id, address, channel, path, blob, mime, dimensions, source, author_session, created, and links
References media by URI scheme and content-addressed blob
concerns: Width and height are 0, which is unusual for an image record
No descriptive alt text or caption beyond the ground-truth label
links array is empty, leaving no explicit relationships
notes: A sparse metadata stub for a design-source presentation slide image, marked as ground truth but missing dimensions and descriptive content.
questions: Why are the image dimensions recorded as 0?
What visual content does p-13 contain?
Is the ground-truth label intended for training, audit, or source-of-truth tracking?
Should the links array connect this asset to slides, pages, or related assets?
purpose_vs_actual: The body says it is a SOURCE design ground-truth record, and the frontmatter does record provenance for an image asset; however, it provides almost no descriptive or relational content, so the ground-truth purpose is only partially realized.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to catalog a specific design-source presentation slide image as authoritative source material, making it discoverable by channel, path, and blob for later retrieval or audit.
novelty: The combination of an image://design-source URI, a blob://b2 content-addressed reference, and a 'SOURCE design ground-truth' label within a single ledger card.
contribution: It contributes a ground-truth metadata record that ties a presentation image to a channel, path, blob, MIME type, author session, and timestamp, which a fused system can keep for provenance and asset lookup.
summary_for_embedding: A ledger markdown file with YAML frontmatter describing a design-source JPEG image named p-13 from presentation-15p, referenced by an image URI and a blob hash, with zero dimensions and a ground-truth caption.
intention_for_embedding: To catalog a specific design-source presentation slide image as ground-truth source material with provenance metadata.
